issues.detail: Clearwater Analytics take-private completed June 25, 2026 at $24.55/share. Australian FIRB granted June 19; deal closed June 25. CWAN delisted; stub still pricing at $24.56. Remove from portfolios.json and record successful merger-arb exit.
issues.action: Remove CWAN from data/portfolios.json growth sleeve.

summary.headline: AI-chip rout: CRDO -10.5%, QCOM/ADI -8%, NXPI/KEYS -7.6% on SK Hynix HBM4 slowdown + Broadcom capex miss; CAT -5.5% as tariff headwinds materialize. CWAN merger-arb closed June 25 at $24.55 — resolved. Hyperscaler late-July prints are next key signal.

flags.headline: CRDO -10.5%: AI capex cycle risk materializing; largest single-day drop in portfolio
flags.detail: Credo Technology fell 10.51% — the largest move in the book — as the AI-infrastructure selloff triggered by SK Hynix announcing HBM4/NAND production slowdown (June 23) and Broadcom's Q3 AI chip guidance miss ($16B vs $17.2B consensus, June 5) hit custom-silicon connectivity names hardest. Despite CRDO's record Q4 revenue (+157% YoY, $437M), the stock had priced in continued hyper-aggressive capex from the major cloud providers; that assumption is now being repriced. Competitive entry from Broadcom in AI connectivity adds a second headwind.
flags.thesis_link: Matches CRDO key_risk: 'AI capex cycle dependency — a slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending due to ROI concerns or macroeconomic headwinds.' Also matches: 'Competitive entry from Broadcom, Marvell, and other large semiconductor companies.' Broadcom's guidance miss is both the macro trigger and the competitive signal.

evidence.source: Intellectia AI — Semiconductor Stocks Selloff June 2026: $1.3T Wiped Out in AI Chip Crash
Kavout — What Triggered the Recent Semiconductor Sell-Off
Seeking Alpha — Credo Q4 revenue surges 157% but fails to satisfy investors

flags.headline: QCOM -8.0%: Memory shortage thesis confirmed; handset concentration headwind
flags.detail: Qualcomm fell 8.0% as the semiconductor sector selloff compounded pre-existing handset weakness. Handset revenues fell 13% YoY in recent quarters. SK Hynix's production slowdown directly impacts the memory supply chain that QCOM identified as constraining handset production. Qualcomm's AI data-center revenue push is not expected to contribute meaningfully until 2028, leaving the name without a near-term offset for handset softness.
flags.thesis_link: Matches QCOM key_risk: 'Global memory shortage constraining handset production — Q2 FY2026 guidance cut reflects supply limitations.' SK Hynix slowing HBM4/NAND production directly hits the supply chain this risk describes. Also matches 'Handset concentration — QCT Handsets at $7.8B quarterly (74% of QCT).'

evidence.source: TradingKey — QCOM Moved Down on Jun 5 (initial Broadcom-driven drop, with follow-on moves through June)
CNBC — Tech rout intensifies as sell-off grips global stocks

flags.headline: KEYS -7.6%: Valuation compression + AI capex dependency risk emerging simultaneously
flags.detail: Keysight fell 7.64% despite record quarterly results and raised guidance. GF Value estimated the stock 66% overvalued at ~$328 vs. a $197.99 intrinsic estimate. The combination of extreme valuation (51x trailing P/E per thesis report) and the emerging narrative that AI test equipment demand could moderate if hyperscaler capex slows is creating multiple compression. This flag is thesis-linked on two dimensions: capex cycle AND valuation.
flags.thesis_link: Matches KEYS key_risks: (1) 'AI capex cycle dependency — any slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending would directly impact Keysight's fastest-growing segment.' (2) 'Valuation compression risk — at 51.2x trailing P/E and 28.3x forward P/E, KEYS requires continued 20%+ growth; any deceleration triggers multiple compression.' Both risks are now manifesting simultaneously.

evidence.source: GuruFocus — Keysight Technologies (KEYS) Shares Fall 8.7% — GF Value Says Still Overvalued

flags.headline: CAT -5.5%: Tariff headwind ($2.2-2.4B FY2026) and Fed hawkishness compressing premium valuation
flags.detail: Caterpillar fell 5.53% from near its all-time high (~$1,057) to $998.18. Drivers: (1) reports projecting $2.2-2.4B in incremental FY2026 tariff costs are being priced into the forward earnings model, (2) stronger-than-expected jobs data raised expectations for prolonged elevated rates, adding rate-sensitivity pressure to an industrial name that had rallied ~60% YTD and was trading at ~48-51x P/E. The thesis key_risk is now visibly materializing in the stock price.
flags.thesis_link: Directly matches CAT key_risk: '$2.6B in incremental tariff costs in 2026 that could compress adjusted operating profit margins below 2025's ~17% despite efficiency efforts.' The market is now pricing in this specific risk. Also relates to 'Premium valuation at 38.8x trailing P/E leaves limited margin of safety if EPS growth fails to materialize.'

evidence.source: TradingKey — Caterpillar (CAT) Moved Down by 4.31% on Jun 26

flags.headline: ADI -8.0%: Sector contagion; no company-specific negative; record Q2 revenue intact
flags.detail: Analog Devices fell 7.99%, the second-largest Dividend-sleeve decline. No company-specific catalyst identified — the move is entirely sector contagion from SK Hynix / Broadcom headlines. ADI posted record Q2 revenue of $3.62B on May 20. The stock's cyclicality risk is a portfolio-level concern, but the underlying thesis (industrial, automotive, and comms recovery) is not directly challenged by HBM4 memory production decisions. Watch for company-specific news before escalating.
flags.thesis_link: Relates to ADI key_risk: 'Semiconductor cyclicality — FY2024's 23% revenue decline from FY2023 peak demonstrates that current 30% growth is partially cyclical recovery.' No specific ADI catalyst — sector-level sentiment only.

evidence.source: TradingKey — ADI Moved Down by 7.56% on Jun 26

flags.headline: NXPI -7.7%: Sector contagion on a HOLD name; automotive recovery thesis intact
flags.detail: NXP Semiconductors fell 7.65% despite Citi raising its price target to $370 the same day — a sign the move was entirely sector-driven rather than company-specific. Q1 2026 revenue came in at $3.18B (+12% YoY). NXPI is already rated HOLD (score 65); the sector shock magnifies an already cautious view. Automotive semiconductor recovery and CEO transition execution remain the two thesis-level watches.
flags.thesis_link: Sector contagion touches NXPI key_risk: 'Automotive semiconductor cyclicality' and 'U.S.-China trade tensions and tariff uncertainty creating demand visibility challenges.' No specific automotive negative emerged; watch for follow-through.

evidence.source: TIKR — NXP Semiconductors Stock Fell 7% the Same Day Citi Raised Target to $370

flags.headline: LRCX -5.9%: SK Hynix HBM4 slowdown directly reduces near-term WFE tool demand
flags.detail: Lam Research fell 5.93% after a ~115% YTD rally. Unlike pure sector contagion, SK Hynix slowing HBM4 production is directly relevant to Lam — it sells the etch and deposition tools used in advanced memory fabs. WFE spending moderation from a major DRAM customer is a real operational headwind. Director Eric Brandt liquidated $19.1M (21.48% stake reduction) added to near-term selling pressure.
flags.thesis_link: Matches LRCX key_risk: 'Semiconductor equipment cyclicality — WFE spending has historically experienced 20-30% peak-to-trough swings.' SK Hynix's production pullback is a direct, not derivative, headwind to Lam's order pipeline.

evidence.source: GuruFocus — Lam Research (LRCX) Stock Down 9.3% but Still Overvalued — GF Score: 85/100

flags.headline: MRVL -5.7%: CFO selling 211K shares + sector weakness on a 91x P/E name
flags.detail: Marvell fell 5.69%, compounded by CFO insider selling (211,329 shares) and a 'sell-the-news' dynamic after S&P 500 inclusion on June 22. At ~91x forward P/E, MRVL is priced for perfection on AI custom silicon. The sector-level AI capex moderation narrative — while not yet confirmed — is a direct risk to the valuation model. Core thesis (hyperscaler custom AI ASICs, data center interconnect) remains intact but the stock has no margin for deceleration.
flags.thesis_link: Matches MRVL key_risk: 'AI capex cycle moderation — if hyperscalers reduce data center investment due to ROI concerns or macro headwinds.' CFO selling is an additional caution signal worth monitoring.

evidence.source: Timothy Sykes — MRVL Stock Slumps As CFO Sale And Chip Weakness Rattle Traders

flags.headline: Late-July hyperscaler earnings — capex commentary is now the highest-priority macro signal for 8 AI-infra holdings
flags.detail: With SK Hynix's HBM4 slowdown (June 23) and Broadcom's guidance miss (June 5) already in the tape, the late-July 2026 hyperscaler earnings prints (Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta) carry elevated importance. Tone on data center capex will either confirm or contradict the current selloff narrative across NVDA, MRVL, AMD, TSM, CRDO, LRCX, KEYS, and ADI. NVDA (-2.0%) is holding up better than the semicon ecosystem names, suggesting the market is not yet pricing a full capex reversal at the market leader — but that could change on any guidance miss.
flags.thesis_link: Standing watch per Risk Sentinel mandate. Per-holding thesis: CRDO, MRVL, KEYS, LRCX all list 'AI capex cycle dependency' as an explicit key_risk.

deal_watch.terms: $24.55 take-private (Permira / Warburg Pincus / Francisco Partners / Temasek)
deal_watch.note: Deal completed June 25 at $24.55/share. Australian FIRB approved June 19. No slippage reported — deal progressed on schedule and closed Q2 2026 as initially anticipated. CWAN delisted from NYSE. Stub still pricing at $24.56 in data drop. Action: remove CWAN from portfolios.json growth sleeve.

calendar.note: Elevated importance given SK Hynix HBM4 slowdown (June 23) and Broadcom capex guidance miss (June 5). Will confirm or refute current selloff narrative. NVDA holding at -2.0% vs. -7-10% for ecosystem names — market not yet pricing a full capex reversal at the leader.
